summary: axois-utils postinstall exfiltrates host/user info over HTTP
details: Package name is a typosquat of the popular `axios` HTTP client (single-character transposition). The package ships a `postinstall.js` lifecycle script that imports `child_process` and `http`, collects host identity fields (`os.hostname()`, username, current working directory) into a structured object (postinstall.js lines 14, 94-98), and POSTs the data via `http.request(...)` (lines 216-217). The combination of a typosquat name, lifecycle-hook execution, system reconnaissance fields, and outbound HTTP transmission matches the canonical npm install-time data-exfiltration pattern. Installing or even running `npm install` on a project that pulls this package will silently send installer host metadata to the operator-controlled endpoint without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
